structure.name: Claim_Related

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify care team entries.

items.name: provider
items.description: Member of the team who provided the product or service.

items.name: responsible
items.description: The party who is billing and/or responsible for the claimed products or services.

items.name: role
items.description: The lead, assisting or supervising practitioner and their discipline if a multidisciplinary team.

items.name: qualification
items.description: The qualification of the practitioner which is applicable for this service.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify supporting information entries.

items.name: category
items.description: The general class of the information supplied: information; exception; accident, employment; onset, etc.

items.name: code
items.description: System and code pertaining to the specific information regarding special conditions relating to the setting, treatment or patient  for which care is sought.

items.name: timing[x]
items.description: The date when or period to which this information refers.

items.name: value[x]
items.description: Additional data or information such as resources, documents, images etc. including references to the data or the actual inclusion of the data.

items.name: reason
items.description: Provides the reason in the situation where a reason code is required in addition to the content.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify diagnosis entries.

items.name: diagnosis[x]
items.description: The nature of illness or problem in a coded form or as a reference to an external defined Condition.

items.name: type
items.description: When the condition was observed or the relative ranking.

items.name: onAdmission
items.description: Indication of whether the diagnosis was present on admission to a facility.

items.name: packageCode
items.description: A package billing code or bundle code used to group products and services to a particular health condition (such as heart attack) which is based on a predetermined grouping code system.

items.name: sequence

items.name: type
items.description: When the condition was observed or the relative ranking.

items.name: date
items.description: Date and optionally time the procedure was performed.

items.name: procedure[x]
items.description: The code or reference to a Procedure resource which identifies the clinical intervention performed.

structure.name: Claim_Insurance
structure.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: id

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify insurance entries and provide a sequence of coverages to convey coordination of benefit order.

items.name: focal
items.description: A flag to indicate that this Coverage is to be used for adjudication of this claim when set to true.

items.name: identifier
items.description: The business identifier to be used when the claim is sent for adjudication against this insurance policy.

items.name: coverage
items.description: Reference to the insurance card level information contained in the Coverage resource. The coverage issuing insurer will use these details to locate the patient's actual coverage within the insurer's information system.

items.name: businessArrangement
items.description: A business agreement number established between the provider and the insurer for special business processing purposes.

items.name: preAuthRef
items.description: Reference numbers previously provided by the insurer to the provider to be quoted on subsequent claims containing services or products related to the prior authorization.

items.name: claimResponse
items.description: The result of the adjudication of the line items for the Coverage specified in this insurance.

structure.name: Claim_Accident

items.name: date
items.description: Date of an accident event  related to the products and services contained in the claim.

items.name: type
items.description: The type or context of the accident event for the purposes of selection of potential insurance coverages and determination of coordination between insurers.

items.name: location[x]
items.description: The physical location of the accident event.

structure.name: Claim_Item_Detail_SubDetail
structure.description: A claim detail line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of sub-details which are simple items.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify item entries.

items.name: revenue
items.description: The type of revenue or cost center providing the product and/or service.

items.name: category
items.description: Code to identify the general type of benefits under which products and services are provided.

items.name: productOrService
items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.name: modifier
items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: programCode
items.description: Identifies the program under which this may be recovered.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: udi
items.description: Unique Device Identifiers associated with this line item.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify item entries.

items.name: careTeamSequence
items.description: CareTeam members related to this service or product.

items.name: diagnosisSequence
items.description: Diagnosis applicable for this service or product.

items.name: procedureSequence
items.description: Procedures applicable for this service or product.

items.name: informationSequence
items.description: Exceptions, special conditions and supporting information applicable for this service or product.

items.name: revenue
items.description: The type of revenue or cost center providing the product and/or service.

items.name: category
items.description: Code to identify the general type of benefits under which products and services are provided.

items.name: productOrService
items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.name: modifier
items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: programCode
items.description: Identifies the program under which this may be recovered.

items.name: serviced[x]
items.description: The date or dates when the service or product was supplied, performed or completed.

items.name: location[x]
items.description: Where the product or service was provided.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: udi
items.description: Unique Device Identifiers associated with this line item.

items.name: bodySite
items.description: Physical service site on the patient (limb, tooth, etc.).

items.name: subSite
items.description: A region or surface of the bodySite, e.g. limb region or tooth surface(s).

items.name: encounter
items.description: The Encounters during which this Claim was created or to which the creation of this record is tightly associated.

items.name: detail
items.description: A claim detail line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of sub-details which are simple items.

structure.name: Claim
structure.description: A provider issued list of professional services and products which have been provided, or are to be provided, to a patient which is sent to an insurer for reimbursement.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: contained
items.description: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: modifierExtension
items.description:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this claim.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: The category of claim, e.g. oral, pharmacy, vision, institutional, professional.

items.name: subType
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: use
items.description: A code to indicate whether the nature of the request is: to request adjudication of products and services previously rendered; or requesting authorization and adjudication for provision in the future; or requesting the non-binding adjudication of the listed products and services which could be provided in the future.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party to whom the professional services and/or products have been supplied or are being considered and for whom actual or forecast reimbursement is sought.

items.name: billablePeriod
items.description: The period for which charges are being submitted.

items.name: created
items.description: The date this resource was created.

items.name: enterer
items.description: Individual who created the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The Insurer who is target of the request.

items.name: provider
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: priority
items.description: The provider-required urgency of processing the request. Typical values include: stat, routine deferred.

items.name: fundsReserve
items.description: A code to indicate whether and for whom funds are to be reserved for future claims.

items.name: related
items.description: Other claims which are related to this claim such as prior submissions or claims for related services or for the same event.

items.name: prescription
items.description: Prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy, device or vision products.

items.name: originalPrescription
items.description: Original prescription which has been superseded by this prescription to support the dispensing of pharmacy services, medications or products.

items.name: payee
items.description: The party to be reimbursed for cost of the products and services according to the terms of the policy.

items.name: referral
items.description: A reference to a referral resource.

items.name: facility
items.description: Facility where the services were provided.

items.name: careTeam
items.description: The members of the team who provided the products and services.

items.name: supportingInfo
items.description: Additional information codes regarding exceptions, special considerations, the condition, situation, prior or concurrent issues.

items.name: diagnosis
items.description: Information about diagnoses relevant to the claim items.

items.name: procedure
items.description: Procedures performed on the patient relevant to the billing items with the claim.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: accident
items.description: Details of an accident which resulted in injuries which required the products and services listed in the claim.

items.name: item
items.description: A claim line. Either a simple  product or service or a 'group' of details which can each be a simple items or groups of sub-details.

items.name: total
items.description: The total value of the all the items in the claim.

structure.name: ClaimResponse_Item_Adjudication
structure.description: If this item is a group then the values here are a summary of the adjudication of the detail items. If this item is a simple product or service then this is the result of the adjudication of this item.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.description:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: category
items.description: A code to indicate the information type of this adjudication record. Information types may include the value submitted, maximum values or percentages allowed or payable under the plan, amounts that: the patient is responsible for in aggregate or pertaining to this item; amounts paid by other coverages; and, the benefit payable for this item.

items.name: reason
items.description: A code supporting the understanding of the adjudication result and explaining variance from expected amount.

items.name: amount
items.description: Monetary amount associated with the category.

items.name: value
items.description: A non-monetary value associated with the category. Mutually exclusive to the amount element above.

structure.name: ClaimResponse_Item_Detail_SubDetail

items.name: productOrService
items.description: When the value is a group code then this item collects a set of related claim details, otherwise this contains the product, service, drug or other billing code for the item.

items.name: modifier
items.description: Item typification or modifiers codes to convey additional context for the product or service.

items.name: quantity
items.description: The number of repetitions of a service or product.

items.name: unitPrice
items.description: If the item is not a group then this is the fee for the product or service, otherwise this is the total of the fees for the details of the group.

items.name: factor
items.description: A real number that represents a multiplier used in determining the overall value of services delivered and/or goods received. The concept of a Factor allows for a discount or surcharge multiplier to be applied to a monetary amount.

items.name: net
items.description: The quantity times the unit price for an additional service or product or charge.

items.name: noteNumber
items.description: The numbers associated with notes below which apply to the adjudication of this item.

items.name: category
items.description: A code to indicate the information type of this adjudication record. Information types may include: the value submitted, maximum values or percentages allowed or payable under the plan, amounts that the patient is responsible for in aggregate or pertaining to this item, amounts paid by other coverages, and the benefit payable for this item.

items.name: type

items.description: Total amount of all adjustments to this payment included in this transaction which are not related to this claim's adjudication.

items.name: adjustmentReason

items.name: date

items.name: amount
items.description: Benefits payable less any payment adjustment.

items.name: identifier
items.description: Issuer's unique identifier for the payment instrument.

items.name: sequence
items.description: A number to uniquely identify insurance entries and provide a sequence of coverages to convey coordination of benefit order.

items.name: focal
items.description: A flag to indicate that this Coverage is to be used for adjudication of this claim when set to true.

items.name: coverage
items.description: Reference to the insurance card level information contained in the Coverage resource. The coverage issuing insurer will use these details to locate the patient's actual coverage within the insurer's information system.

items.name: businessArrangement
items.description: A business agreement number established between the provider and the insurer for special business processing purposes.

items.name: claimResponse
items.description: The result of the adjudication of the line items for the Coverage specified in this insurance.

items.name: itemSequence
items.description: The sequence number of the line item submitted which contains the error. This value is omitted when the error occurs outside of the item structure.

items.name: detailSequence
items.description: The sequence number of the detail within the line item submitted which contains the error. This value is omitted when the error occurs outside of the item structure.

items.name: subDetailSequence
items.description: The sequence number of the sub-detail within the detail within the line item submitted which contains the error. This value is omitted when the error occurs outside of the item structure.

items.name: code
items.description: An error code, from a specified code system, which details why the claim could not be adjudicated.

structure.name: ClaimResponse
structure.description: This resource provides the adjudication details from the processing of a Claim resource.

items.name: id
items.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

items.name: meta
items.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

items.name: implicitRules
items.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

items.name: language
items.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

items.name: text
items.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

items.name: contained
items.description: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. items.name: identifier
items.description: A unique identifier assigned to this claim response.

items.name: status
items.description: The status of the resource instance.

items.name: type
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: subType
items.description: A finer grained suite of claim type codes which may convey additional information such as Inpatient vs Outpatient and/or a specialty service.

items.name: use
items.description: A code to indicate whether the nature of the request is: to request adjudication of products and services previously rendered; or requesting authorization and adjudication for provision in the future; or requesting the non-binding adjudication of the listed products and services which could be provided in the future.

items.name: patient
items.description: The party to whom the professional services and/or products have been supplied or are being considered and for whom actual for facast reimbursement is sought.

items.name: created
items.description: The date this resource was created.

items.name: insurer
items.description: The party responsible for authorization, adjudication and reimbursement.

items.name: requestor
items.description: The provider which is responsible for the claim, predetermination or preauthorization.

items.name: request
items.description: Original request resource reference.

items.name: outcome
items.description: The outcome of the claim, predetermination, or preauthorization processing.

items.name: disposition
items.description: A human readable description of the status of the adjudication.

items.name: preAuthRef
items.description: Reference from the Insurer which is used in later communications which refers to this adjudication.

items.name: preAuthPeriod
items.description: The time frame during which this authorization is effective.

items.name: payeeType
items.description: Type of Party to be reimbursed: subscriber, provider, other.

items.name: item
items.description: A claim line. Either a simple (a product or service) or a 'group' of details which can also be a simple items or groups of sub-details.

items.name: addItem
items.description: The first-tier service adjudications for payor added product or service lines.

items.name: adjudication
items.description: The adjudication results which are presented at the header level rather than at the line-item or add-item levels.

items.name: total
items.description: Categorized monetary totals for the adjudication.

items.name: payment
items.description: Payment details for the adjudication of the claim.

items.name: fundsReserve
items.description: A code, used only on a response to a preauthorization, to indicate whether the benefits payable have been reserved and for whom.

items.name: formCode
items.description: A code for the form to be used for printing the content.

items.name: form
items.description: The actual form, by reference or inclusion, for printing the content or an EOB.

items.name: processNote
items.description: A note that describes or explains adjudication results in a human readable form.

items.name: communicationRequest
items.description: Request for additional supporting or authorizing information.

items.name: insurance
items.description: Financial instruments for reimbursement for the health care products and services specified on the claim.

items.name: error
items.description: Errors encountered during the processing of the adjudication.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Slicing_Discriminator
structure.description: Designates which child elements are used to discriminate between the slices when processing an instance. If one or more discriminators are provided, the value of the child elements in the instance data SHALL completely distinguish which slice the element in the resource matches based on the allowed values for those elements in each of the slices.

items.name: type
items.description: How the element value is interpreted when discrimination is evaluated.

items.name: path
items.description: A FHIRPath expression, using [the simple subset of FHIRPath](fhirpath.html#simple), that is used to identify the element on which discrimination is based.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Slicing
structure.description: Indicates that the element is sliced into a set of alternative definitions (i.e. in a structure definition, there are multiple different constraints on a single element in the base resource). Slicing can be used in any resource that has cardinality ..* on the base resource, or any resource with a choice of types. The set of slices is any elements that come after this in the element sequence that have the same path, until a shorter path occurs (the shorter path terminates the set).

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: discriminator
items.description: Designates which child elements are used to discriminate between the slices when processing an instance. If one or more discriminators are provided, the value of the child elements in the instance data SHALL completely distinguish which slice the element in the resource matches based on the allowed values for those elements in each of the slices.

items.name: description
items.description: A human-readable text description of how the slicing works. If there is no discriminator, this is required to be present to provide whatever information is possible about how the slices can be differentiated.

items.name: ordered
items.description: If the matching elements have to occur in the same order as defined in the profile.

items.name: rules
items.description: Whether additional slices are allowed or not. When the slices are ordered, profile authors can also say that additional slices are only allowed at the end.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Base
structure.description: Information about the base definition of the element, provided to make it unnecessary for tools to trace the deviation of the element through the derived and related profiles. When the element definition is not the original definition of an element - i.g. either in a constraint on another type, or for elements from a super type in a snap shot - then the information in provided in the element definition may be different to the base definition. On the original definition of the element, it will be same.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: path
items.description: The Path that identifies the base element - this matches the ElementDefinition.path for that element. Across FHIR, there is only one base definition of any element - that is, an element definition on a [StructureDefinition](structuredefinition.html#) without a StructureDefinition.base.

items.name: min
items.description: Minimum cardinality of the base element identified by the path.

items.name: max
items.description: Maximum cardinality of the base element identified by the path.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Type
structure.description: The data type or resource that the value of this element is permitted to be.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: code
items.description: URL of Data type or Resource that is a(or the) type used for this element. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition e.g. "string" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/string. Absolute URLs are only allowed in logical models.

items.name: profile
items.description: Identifies a profile structure or implementation Guide that applies to the datatype this element refers to. If any profiles are specified, then the content must conform to at least one of them. The URL can be a local reference - to a contained StructureDefinition, or a reference to another StructureDefinition or Implementation Guide by a canonical URL. When an implementation guide is specified, the type SHALL conform to at least one profile defined in the implementation guide.

items.name: targetProfile
items.description: Used when the type is "Reference" or "canonical", and identifies a profile structure or implementation Guide that applies to the target of the reference this element refers to. If any profiles are specified, then the content must conform to at least one of them. The URL can be a local reference - to a contained StructureDefinition, or a reference to another StructureDefinition or Implementation Guide by a canonical URL. When an implementation guide is specified, the target resource SHALL conform to at least one profile defined in the implementation guide.

items.name: aggregation
items.description: If the type is a reference to another resource, how the resource is or can be aggregated - is it a contained resource, or a reference, and if the context is a bundle, is it included in the bundle.

items.name: versioning
items.description: Whether this reference needs to be version specific or version independent, or whether either can be used.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Example
structure.description: A sample value for this element demonstrating the type of information that would typically be found in the element.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: label
items.description: Describes the purpose of this example amoung the set of examples.

items.name: value[x]
items.description: The actual value for the element, which must be one of the types allowed for this element.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Constraint
structure.description: Formal constraints such as co-occurrence and other constraints that can be computationally evaluated within the context of the instance.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: key
items.description: Allows identification of which elements have their cardinalities impacted by the constraint.  Will not be referenced for constraints that do not affect cardinality.

items.name: requirements
items.description: Description of why this constraint is necessary or appropriate.

items.name: severity
items.description: Identifies the impact constraint violation has on the conformance of the instance.

items.name: human
items.description: Text that can be used to describe the constraint in messages identifying that the constraint has been violated.

items.name: expression
items.description: A [FHIRPath](fhirpath.html) expression of constraint that can be executed to see if this constraint is met.

items.name: xpath
items.description: An XPath expression of constraint that can be executed to see if this constraint is met.

items.name: source
items.description: A reference to the original source of the constraint, for traceability purposes.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Binding
structure.description: Binds to a value set if this element is coded (code, Coding, CodeableConcept, Quantity), or the data types (string, uri).

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: strength
items.description: Indicates the degree of conformance expectations associated with this binding - that is, the degree to which the provided value set must be adhered to in the instances.

items.name: description
items.description: Describes the intended use of this particular set of codes.

items.name: valueSet
items.description: Refers to the value set that identifies the set of codes the binding refers to.

structure.name: ElementDefinition_Mapping
structure.description: Identifies a concept from an external specification that roughly corresponds to this element.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: identity
items.description: An internal reference to the definition of a mapping.

items.name: language
items.description: Identifies the computable language in which mapping.map is expressed.

items.name: map
items.description: Expresses what part of the target specification corresponds to this element.

items.name: comment
items.description: Comments that provide information about the mapping or its use.

structure.name: ElementDefinition
structure.description: Base StructureDefinition for ElementDefinition Type: Captures constraints on each element within the resource, profile, or extension.

items.name: id
items.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

items.name: extension
items.description: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

items.name: modifierExtension
items.description:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

items.name: path
items.description: The path identifies the element and is expressed as a "."-separated list of ancestor elements, beginning with the name of the resource or extension.

items.name: representation
items.description: Codes that define how this element is represented in instances, when the deviation varies from the normal case.

items.name: sliceName
items.description: The name of this element definition slice, when slicing is working. The name must be a token with no dots or spaces. This is a unique name referring to a specific set of constraints applied to this element, used to provide a name to different slices of the same element.

items.name: sliceIsConstraining
items.description: If true, indicates that this slice definition is constraining a slice definition with the same name in an inherited profile. If false, the slice is not overriding any slice in an inherited profile. If missing, the slice might or might not be overriding a slice in an inherited profile, depending on the sliceName.

items.name: label
items.description: A single preferred label which is the text to display beside the element indicating its meaning or to use to prompt for the element in a user display or form.

items.name: code
items.description: A code that has the same meaning as the element in a particular terminology.

items.name: slicing
items.description: Indicates that the element is sliced into a set of alternative definitions (i.e. in a structure definition, there are multiple different constraints on a single element in the base resource). Slicing can be used in any resource that has cardinality ..* on the base resource, or any resource with a choice of types. The set of slices is any elements that come after this in the element sequence that have the same path, until a shorter path occurs (the shorter path terminates the set).

items.name: short
items.description: A concise description of what this element means (e.g. for use in autogenerated summaries).

items.name: definition
items.description: Provides a complete explanation of the meaning of the data element for human readability.  For the case of elements derived from existing elements (e.g. constraints), the definition SHALL be consistent with the base definition, but convey the meaning of the element in the particular context of use of the resource. (Note: The text you are reading is specified in ElementDefinition.definition).

items.name: comment
items.description: Explanatory notes and implementation guidance about the data element, including notes about how to use the data properly, exceptions to proper use, etc. (Note: The text you are reading is specified in ElementDefinition.comment).

items.name: requirements
items.description: This element is for traceability of why the element was created and why the constraints exist as they do. This may be used to point to source materials or specifications that drove the structure of this element.

items.name: alias
items.description: Identifies additional names by which this element might also be known.

items.name: min
items.description: The minimum number of times this element SHALL appear in the instance.

items.name: max
items.description: The maximum number of times this element is permitted to appear in the instance.

items.name: base
items.description: Information about the base definition of the element, provided to make it unnecessary for tools to trace the deviation of the element through the derived and related profiles. When the element definition is not the original definition of an element - i.g. either in a constraint on another type, or for elements from a super type in a snap shot - then the information in provided in the element definition may be different to the base definition. On the original definition of the element, it will be same.

items.name: contentReference
items.description: Identifies an element defined elsewhere in the definition whose content rules should be applied to the current element. ContentReferences bring across all the rules that are in the ElementDefinition for the element, including definitions, cardinality constraints, bindings, invariants etc.

items.name: type
items.description: The data type or resource that the value of this element is permitted to be.

items.name: defaultValue[x]
items.description: The value that should be used if there is no value stated in the instance (e.g. 'if not otherwise specified, the abstract is false').

items.name: meaningWhenMissing
items.description: The Implicit meaning that is to be understood when this element is missing (e.g. 'when this element is missing, the period is ongoing').

items.name: orderMeaning
items.description: If present, indicates that the order of the repeating element has meaning and describes what that meaning is.  If absent, it means that the order of the element has no meaning.

items.name: fixed[x]
items.description: Specifies a value that SHALL be exactly the value  for this element in the instance. For purposes of comparison, non-significant whitespace is ignored, and all values must be an exact match (case and accent sensitive). Missing elements/attributes must also be missing.

items.name: pattern[x]
items.description:
Specifies a value that the value in the instance SHALL follow - that is, any value in the pattern must be found in the instance. Other additional values may be found too. This is effectively constraint by example.  

When pattern[x] is used to constrain a primitive, it means that the value provided in the pattern[x] must match the instance value exactly.

When pattern[x] is used to constrain an array, it means that each element provided in the pattern[x] array must (recursively) match at least one element from the instance array.

When pattern[x] is used to constrain a complex object, it means that each property in the pattern must be present in the complex object, and its value must recursively match -- i.e.,

1. If primitive: it must match exactly the pattern value
2. If a complex object: it must match (recursively) the pattern value
3. If an array: it must match (recursively) the pattern value.

items.name: example
items.description: A sample value for this element demonstrating the type of information that would typically be found in the element.

items.name: minValue[x]
items.description: The minimum allowed value for the element. The value is inclusive. This is allowed for the types date, dateTime, instant, time, decimal, integer, and Quantity.

items.name: maxValue[x]
items.description: The maximum allowed value for the element. The value is inclusive. This is allowed for the types date, dateTime, instant, time, decimal, integer, and Quantity.

items.name: maxLength
items.description: Indicates the maximum length in characters that is permitted to be present in conformant instances and which is expected to be supported by conformant consumers that support the element.

items.name: condition
items.description: A reference to an invariant that may make additional statements about the cardinality or value in the instance.

items.name: constraint
items.description: Formal constraints such as co-occurrence and other constraints that can be computationally evaluated within the context of the instance.

items.name: mustSupport
items.description: If true, implementations that produce or consume resources SHALL provide "support" for the element in some meaningful way.  If false, the element may be ignored and not supported. If false, whether to populate or use the data element in any way is at the discretion of the implementation.

items.name: isModifier
items.description: If true, the value of this element affects the interpretation of the element or resource that contains it, and the value of the element cannot be ignored. Typically, this is used for status, negation and qualification codes. The effect of this is that the element cannot be ignored by systems: they SHALL either recognize the element and process it, and/or a pre-determination has been made that it is not relevant to their particular system.

items.name: isModifierReason
items.description: Explains how that element affects the interpretation of the resource or element that contains it.

items.name: isSummary
items.description: Whether the element should be included if a client requests a search with the parameter _summary=true.

items.name: binding
items.description: Binds to a value set if this element is coded (code, Coding, CodeableConcept, Quantity), or the data types (string, uri).

items.name: mapping
items.description: Identifies a concept from an external specification that roughly corresponds to this element.
